title: Miniature Headphone Materials Demo Diorama
prompt:
Create a single HTML file with an interactive 3D headphone materials demo diorama using Three.js and GLTFLoader. Load the model 'facecap.glb' as the centerpiece mannequin head for displaying headphone material concepts.

SCENE SETUP:
- Create a circular diorama platform (radius 3, height 0.3) with a dark wood-textured material (use procedural: dark brown MeshStandardMaterial with roughness 0.8).
- Load 'facecap.glb' and place it centered on the platform, scaled so it fits nicely (approximately scale 2.0). Position the model at y=0.3 (on top of platform).
- Add a rotating turntable: the entire platform + model should slowly auto-rotate around Y axis at 0.3 rad/s. This auto-rotation can be toggled.
- Camera: PerspectiveCamera at position (0, 4, 7), looking at (0, 2, 0). Add OrbitControls.
- Lighting: One warm DirectionalLight (color 0xFFE4C4, intensity 1.2) from top-right (5,8,5), one cool fill DirectionalLight (color 0xB0C4DE, intensity 0.6) from left (-5,3,-3), and an AmbientLight (0x404040, intensity 0.5). Add a SpotLight (0xFFFFFF, intensity 1.5) from above (0, 10, 0) pointing down with angle Math.PI/6.

MATERIAL SWATCHES:
- Create 5 floating material swatch spheres (radius 0.3) arranged in an arc above and behind the head model at y=4, spread from x=-3 to x=3.
- Materials: (0) 'Leather' - dark brown, roughness 0.7, metalness 0.0; (1) 'Brushed Aluminum' - silver/gray, roughness 0.3, metalness 0.95; (2) 'Matte Plastic' - black, roughness 0.9, metalness 0.0; (3) 'Carbon Fiber' - dark gray, roughness 0.4, metalness 0.3; (4) 'Rose Gold' - color 0xB76E79, roughness 0.2, metalness 0.9.
- Each swatch sphere should gently bob up and down (sine wave, amplitude 0.15, different phase offsets).

INTERACTION:
- Clicking a swatch sphere applies that material to the loaded facecap model (all its meshes). Track which material index is active.
- Add 5 DOM buttons at the bottom of the screen labeled with material names. Clicking a button does the same as clicking the corresponding swatch sphere. The active button should have a highlighted border (3px solid #FFD700).
- Press 'R' to toggle auto-rotation on/off.
- Press 'E' to toggle an 'explode' view: the swatch spheres spread outward by 1.5x their positions and the model scales up by 1.3x. Toggle back returns to normal.
- Press 'L' to cycle through 3 lighting presets: 'Warm Studio' (default as described), 'Cool Showroom' (swap warm/cool light intensities and colors to bluish tones), 'Dramatic' (kill ambient and fill, boost spot to 3.0 intensity).
- Add a small HUD in the top-left showing: current material name, current lighting preset name, rotation status (ON/OFF), and explode status (ON/OFF).

STATE MANAGEMENT:
- Expose window.__3D_STATE__ with these fields:
  - modelLoaded: boolean, true once facecap.glb is loaded
  - activeMaterialIndex: number (0-4), default 0
  - activeMaterialName: string, e.g. 'Leather'
  - autoRotate: boolean, default true
  - rotationAngle: number, current Y rotation of platform in radians
  - explodeMode: boolean, default false
  - lightingPreset: string, one of 'Warm Studio', 'Cool Showroom', 'Dramatic'
  - lightingPresetIndex: number (0, 1, or 2)
  - swatchCount: number (should be 5)
  - platformVisible: boolean
  - spotLightIntensity: number
  - cameraDistance: number (distance from camera to origin)

Update __3D_STATE__ every frame in the animation loop.
physics_constraints: Auto-rotation should be smooth and continuous at 0.3 rad/s when enabled. Swatch bobbing should follow sinusoidal motion. Explode mode should scale positions proportionally. No NaN values in any state fields.
